p.seg: To all who shall view or hear this writing Richard of Banchory sends greeting.

Know me to have quitclaimed for myself and for my heirs for ever to my lord, Robert de Brus, and his heirs, the entire land of Locharwoods by these boundaries, namely, from 'Polhulin' to 'Blakebecke', just as it extends halfway along the mosse, and from the said middle of the mosse to the Water of Lochar; with pertinents, with a common pasture in the feu of Comlongon which the men of the said Robert de Brus of Mouswald used to have at ferme from me, in return for one merk to be paid yearly, reserving to myself and my heirs in my wood of ..., just as before the day of this quitclaim
In testimony of this matter I have applied my seal to this writing.
